issues.title: Coverage: Users: loans (list, create, get, renew, change due date)
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Users
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** M (1–3 days)

## Background
Add coverage for the user loans resource (list, create, get, renew, change
due date). The audit (2026-05-01) flagged that the original
`create_user_loan` signature mis-modelled the endpoint by mixing query and
body parameters into one flat list and by omitting the alternative
`item_pid` identifier path.

## Relevant Alma API documentation
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Endpoints touched:
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/loans`
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/loans`
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/loans/{loan_id}`
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/loans/{loan_id}` (`op=renew`)
  - `PUT /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/loans/{loan_id}`

Per docs:
- For `POST /loans` (create), **one of** `item_barcode` or `item_pid` is
  **mandatory** as a **query** parameter. `user_id_type` is an optional
  query param.
- The `library` and `circ_desk` fields belong inside the **request body**
  (Loan object), not as flat function args at the same level as
  `item_barcode`.
- `op=renew` is the only documented operation for `POST /loans/{loan_id}`.
- `PUT /loans/{loan_id}` updates the loan (e.g., due date) directly.

## Current wrapper behavior
None of the loans endpoints are wrapped today.

## Expected wrapper behavior

```python
def list_user_loans(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    limit: int = 100,
    offset: int = 0,
    expand: str = None,
    order_by: str = None,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Returns the Alma envelope (`total_record_count`, `item_loan[]`)."""

def create_user_loan(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    *,
    item_barcode: str = None,
    item_pid: str = None,
    user_id_type: str = None,
    loan_data: Dict[str, Any],   # body — must include library + circ_desk
) -> AlmaResponse:
    """
    Exactly one of `item_barcode` / `item_pid` is required (query param).
    `loan_data` is the Loan object body (library, circ_desk, due_date, etc.).
    """

def get_user_loan(self, user_id: str, loan_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: ...

def renew_user_loan(self, user_id: str, loan_id: str) -> AlmaResponse:
    """POST .../loans/{loan_id}?op=renew"""

def change_loan_due_date(
    self, user_id: str, loan_id: str, new_due_date: str,
) -> AlmaResponse:
    """PUT .../loans/{loan_id} with the due_date field updated."""
```

## Implementation notes
- Validate that exactly one of `item_barcode` / `item_pid` is provided in
  `create_user_loan`; raise `AlmaValidationError` otherwise.
- Validate that `loan_data` includes `library` and `circ_desk`; raise
  `AlmaValidationError` with a precise message listing what's missing.
- `new_due_date` must be ISO 8601 with `Z` (e.g., `2026-12-31Z`); reuse the
  date-formatting pattern from `Acquisitions._format_invoice_date` if a
  `datetime` is passed in.
- `op=renew` is a query parameter on the action POST — don't put it in the
  body.
- For PUT due-date updates, do a GET first if the body shape requires the
  full Loan object (verify against docs).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `list_user_loans` returns the Alma envelope and supports
      `limit`/`offset`/`expand`/`order_by` query params.
- [ ] `create_user_loan` accepts `item_barcode` OR `item_pid` (exclusive),
      plus `user_id_type` and a separate `loan_data` body dict; validates
      mutual exclusivity and required body fields.
- [ ] `renew_user_loan` posts to `/loans/{loan_id}?op=renew` with no body.
- [ ] `change_loan_due_date` performs a PUT with a properly formatted ISO
      8601 date string.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: missing/both `item_barcode`+`item_pid` raises
      `AlmaValidationError`; missing body fields raise `AlmaValidationError`;
      `op=renew` is wired correctly; date formatting works for `str` and
      `datetime` inputs.
- [ ] At least one SANDBOX integration test for the full create→renew→PUT
      cycle.
- [ ] No `print()` — all operations logged via `alma_logging`.
- [ ] `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` pass.

## Open questions
- Whether `change_loan_due_date` requires a full Loan object body (round-trip
  GET first) or accepts a partial PUT — verify in docs before merge.

## Audit notes (2026-05-01)
- Primary audit: **Partially aligned**. Endpoints correct, but
  `create_user_loan(user_id, item_barcode, library, circ_desk)` flattens
  query and body params and omits the alternative `item_pid` plus optional
  `user_id_type`.
- Codex direct audit: **Partially aligned** — same finding (`library` and
  `circ_desk` are not documented as parameters to this endpoint outside the
  body).
- Decision: re-shape `create_user_loan` so query params (`item_barcode` /
  `item_pid` / `user_id_type`) are explicit kwargs and body fields go
  through a dedicated `loan_data` dict.

## Files to touch
- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py` (extend existing `Users` class)
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`

## References
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules — `op=renew` query param for renewals

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- POST /loans/{loan_id} uses the `op` query parameter — `op=renew` triggers a renewal.
- PUT /loans/{loan_id} updates the due date directly.
- Date format is ISO 8601 with Z (e.g., `2026-12-31Z`); reuse `Acquisitions._format_invoice_date` pattern.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.

`change_loan_due_date` performs a PUT with a properly formatted ISO
At least one SANDBOX integration test for the full create→renew→PUT
